principles: every palette cites a real-world reference
every palette includes one deliberately wrong colour
roles are named for their world, not primary/secondary/accent
rules and forbidden pairings are explicit
each palette refuses to be universal

palettes.name: Telex
palettes.source: Olivetti house style · Walter Ballmer · mid-80s

muddy.role: footnote, deep ink — the wrong colour

rules: stamp appears once per page
ink on paper is the only body combination
muddy is reserved for footnotes and page numbers
no gradients · no drop shadows

palettes.name: F-91W
palettes.source: Casio F-91W LCD readout · panel + digit colour, photographed at noon

amber.role: backlight — only when held — the wrong colour

rules: amber only appears on press / hold
all numerals monospace, segment-style preferred
ghost segments visible behind active digits
no anti-aliasing on the segments

palettes.name: Shoji
palettes.source: Japanese minka interior · winter light through rice paper

shu.role: vermilion seal — once · the wrong colour

rules: shu appears only as a seal mark
ai is a wall, never a line
sumi for type, timber for structure — they do not mix
negative space is itself a colour

palettes.name: Riso Bisque
palettes.source: Risograph zine palette · fluorescent pink + federal blue on recycled stock

yellow.role: second pass — only when fluo is absent · the wrong colour

rules: two passes only — fluo + federal, or fluo + yellow
always show registration drift (1–2px)
halftone dots visible · do not anti-alias
stock colour bleeds through · never fill 100%

palettes.name: Night Tube
palettes.source: TfL Night Tube launch · sodium-lit platform photography

wine.role: deep warning red — last train · the wrong colour

rules: sodium illuminates only one element per screen
wine reserved for last-call / shutdown states
type is chalk on midnight — never inverted
no fluorescent anything

palettes.name: Kodachrome
palettes.source: Kodachrome 25/64 slide stock · National Geographic colour rendition · 1960–80

pearl.role: mat-board mount · the wrong colour — only unsaturated note

rules: never adjacent: cobalt and butter — they vibrate
vermillion never on velvia
pearl is for mounting only — never ground or type
max two saturated colours per surface
